questions.q: In which industry was M. G. Ramachandran (MGR) a leading film star?
questions.explanation: M. G. Ramachandran was a leading star of Tamil cinema in the 1950s and 1960s.

questions.q: By what popular initials was the Tamil actor and politician M. G. Ramachandran widely known?
questions.explanation: M. G. Ramachandran was universally known by his initials, MGR.

questions.q: Which political party did M. G. Ramachandran found in 1972?
questions.explanation: MGR founded the All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK) in 1972.

questions.q: Of which Indian state did M. G. Ramachandran become Chief Minister in 1977?
questions.explanation: MGR became Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu in 1977.

questions.q: Which of India's highest civilian honours was posthumously awarded to M. G. Ramachandran?
questions.explanation: MGR was posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour.

questions.q: In which year was the Tamil star and politician M. G. Ramachandran born?
questions.explanation: M. G. Ramachandran was born in 1917.

questions.q: In which year did the Tamil actor and Chief Minister M. G. Ramachandran die?
questions.explanation: MGR died in 1987 while serving as Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: What kind of on-screen roles built M. G. Ramachandran's immense fan following in Tamil cinema?
questions.explanation: MGR's heroic, socially-themed roles built an immense fan following.

questions.q: Until what point in time did M. G. Ramachandran serve as Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: MGR served as Chief Minister from 1977 until his death in 1987.

questions.q: During which decades was M. G. Ramachandran a leading star of Tamil cinema?
questions.explanation: MGR was a leading Tamil star of the 1950s and 1960s.

questions.q: Which 1954 Tamil film, in which M. G. Ramachandran starred, is often cited as his first major box-office success?
questions.explanation: Malaikkallan (1954) was a major box-office success that boosted MGR's stardom.

questions.q: Which Dravidian political party did M. G. Ramachandran belong to before founding his own AIADMK?
questions.explanation: MGR was a prominent member of the DMK before splitting away to found the AIADMK in 1972.

questions.q: M. G. Ramachandran's AIADMK was formed after his break with which DMK leader?
questions.explanation: MGR founded the AIADMK in 1972 after falling out with DMK leader M. Karunanidhi.

questions.q: Which female co-star, who later became a Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, frequently appeared opposite M. G. Ramachandran in films?
questions.explanation: Jayalalithaa was a frequent leading lady opposite MGR and later became Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: In which present-day country was M. G. Ramachandran born?
questions.explanation: MGR was born in Nawalapitiya, in present-day Sri Lanka (then Ceylon).

questions.q: The landmark welfare scheme launched during M. G. Ramachandran's tenure as Chief Minister provided free midday meals to which group?
questions.explanation: MGR launched the landmark Nutritious Midday Meal Scheme providing free meals to schoolchildren across Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Before becoming a film star, M. G. Ramachandran began his performing career in which form of theatre?
questions.explanation: MGR began performing as a child in a Tamil drama troupe known as a Boys Company before entering films.

questions.q: After M. G. Ramachandran's death in 1987, which of his protégés eventually led the AIADMK and became Chief Minister?
questions.explanation: Jayalalithaa, MGR's frequent co-star and political protégé, went on to lead the AIADMK and become Chief Minister.

questions.q: Which lifelong rival, a celebrated Tamil 'method' actor, was often contrasted with M. G. Ramachandran in 1950s-60s Tamil cinema?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan and MGR were the two towering rivals of Tamil cinema during the 1950s and 1960s.

questions.q: M. G. Ramachandran's screen image was strongly shaped by which recurring character type?
options: A righteous defender of the common man
questions.explanation: MGR cultivated an enduring screen image as a righteous hero who championed the poor and the common man.

questions.q: What does the 'AIA' part of M. G. Ramachandran's party name, AIADMK, stand for?
questions.explanation: AIADMK stands for All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam, the 'Anna' honouring C. N. Annadurai.

questions.q: By which Tamil honorific, meaning 'Revolutionary Leader', was M. G. Ramachandran popularly hailed by his followers?
questions.explanation: MGR was widely hailed by the honorific 'Puratchi Thalaivar', meaning 'Revolutionary Leader' in Tamil.

questions.q: What was M. G. Ramachandran's full birth name?
questions.explanation: MGR's full name was Marudur Gopalan Ramachandran.

questions.q: In which Tamil-majority town of present-day Sri Lanka was M. G. Ramachandran born in 1917?
questions.explanation: MGR was born in Nawalapitiya, in the Kandy district of present-day Sri Lanka.

questions.q: Which 1965 swashbuckler film starring M. G. Ramachandran and Jayalalithaa became one of his most celebrated pirate-adventure successes?
questions.explanation: Aayirathil Oruvan (1965), starring MGR and Jayalalithaa, was a hugely popular swashbuckling pirate-adventure that stands out for its scale and colour.

questions.q: M. G. Ramachandran survived a notorious 1967 attack in which fellow Tamil actor M. R. Radha did what to him?
questions.explanation: In January 1967 actor M. R. Radha shot MGR, who survived the gunshot wound to the neck and recovered.

questions.q: Which 1973 M. G. Ramachandran film, an Indo-foreign collaboration partly shot abroad, was notable for its globe-trotting adventure setting?
questions.explanation: Ulagam Sutrum Valiban (1973) was an MGR adventure film whose title means 'The Youth Who Travels the World', shot in part overseas.

questions.q: The 1958 film Nadodi Mannan was significant in M. G. Ramachandran's career mainly because he also did what on it?
questions.explanation: Nadodi Mannan (1958) was the first film MGR produced and directed, becoming a landmark blockbuster of his career.

questions.q: Which playback singer was most closely associated with providing the singing voice for M. G. Ramachandran in many of his films?
questions.explanation: T. M. Soundararajan was the playback voice for MGR in a vast number of his Tamil films.

questions.q: M. G. Ramachandran won the National Film Award for Best Actor for his performance in which 1971 (released 1972) film?
questions.explanation: MGR won the National Film Award for Best Actor for Rickshawkaran, in which he played a rickshaw puller.
